expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user to find Sarah Chen's account ID before creating
expectations.evidence: Does not mention jira.mjs. Describes REST API call 'GET /rest/api/3/user/search?query=Sarah%20Chen' but not the skill's lookup-user command.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs create with --project PLATFORM --type Bug
expectations.evidence: Does not mention jira.mjs. Shows a REST API POST to /rest/api/3/issue with project key PLATFORM and issuetype Bug in JSON payload.

expectations.text: Includes --priority High in the create command
expectations.evidence: No jira.mjs command used. The JSON payload does include '"priority": { "name": "High" }' but not as a --priority flag.

expectations.text: Includes --labels flag with backend and urgent
expectations.evidence: No jira.mjs command used. The JSON payload includes '"labels": ["backend", "urgent"]' but not as a --labels flag.

expectations.text: Includes --assignee with the looked-up account ID (not a name)
expectations.evidence: No jira.mjs command used. The JSON payload includes '"assignee": { "accountId": "<SARAH_CHEN_ACCOUNT_ID>" }' with a placeholder, not a looked-up ID.

expectations.text: Writes a concise summary following [Component] [Error] - [Symptom] pattern or similar
expectations.evidence: Summary is 'Login endpoint times out after 30s when user has 50+ active sessions' which follows an [Endpoint] [Error] - [Condition] pattern.

expectations.text: Includes a detailed description mentioning the 30-second timeout and 50 active sessions
expectations.evidence: Description mentions 'times out after 30 seconds when the authenticating user has more than 50 active sessions' with steps to reproduce.

expectations.text: Shows a confirmation step before creating the ticket
expectations.evidence: No confirmation step is described. The approach only shows API payloads for manual execution.
